task: I’m setting up a simple intake and follow-up note for a small hands-on practice, and I want it to be easy to use on busy days. Can you help me draft a basic version with the main points to include and a few plain, professional phrases for recording what happened?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, easy-to-use intake and follow-up note format you can use on busy days.

## Basic intake note
**Client name:**  
**Date:**  
**Time:**  
**Type of visit:**  
**Main reason for visit:**  
**Area(s) addressed:**  
**Anything important to note before starting:**  
- Pain, sensitivity, recent changes, injuries, medications, or preferences

## Basic follow-up note
**What was done:**  
**Client response:**  
**Any changes noticed:**  
**Recommendations / next steps:**  
**Follow-up needed:**  
**Next appointment date (if set):**

## Plain professional phrases you can use

### For intake
- “Client reports mild/moderate/severe discomfort in the ___ area.”
- “Client states symptoms have been present for ___.”
- “Client reports no major changes since last visit.”
- “Client notes increased tension/stiffness/sensitivity.”
- “Client prefers lighter pressure in this area.”
- “Client denies any new concerns.”

### For what happened during the visit
- “Focused work completed on the ___ area.”
- “Attention given to areas of tension and restriction.”
- “Session adjusted based on client feedback.”
- “Pressure was modified for comfort.”
- “Client tolerated the session well.”
- “Shorter session completed due to time constraints.”

### For response / outcome
- “Client reported improvement during the session.”
- “Client noted reduced tension after treatment.”
- “Area felt less restricted following work.”
- “Client responded well to the session.”
- “No unusual response noted.”
- “Client advised to monitor symptoms and follow up as needed.”

## Very short example note
**Intake:**  
Client reports tightness in upper back and shoulders. No new concerns. Prefers moderate pressure.

**Follow-up:**  
Focused work completed on upper back, shoulders, and neck. Client tolerated session well and reported decreased tension afterward. Advised to monitor and return as needed.

If you want, I can also turn this into a **one-line fill-in template** you can copy and reuse for every client visit.

user_simulator.text: Pretty good, but a little too broad. I’d want it tighter for actual use in the room — maybe a version that fits on one small page, with a few fields I’d really need for a hands-on practice: contraindications, consent, pressure preference, areas to avoid, and aftercare. Also, a couple phrases that sound more natural in a chart note. This is close, just needs to be more practical and less generic.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, more practical version you could actually use in the room on a small page.

## Simple intake / follow-up note for hands-on sessions

**Date:**  
**Client:**  
**Main concern / reason for visit:**  
**Contraindications / precautions:**  
- ☐ none reported  
- ☐ yes: __________________

**Consent obtained:**  
- ☐ verbal consent  
- ☐ written consent  
- ☐ client understands session may be stopped at any time

**Pressure preference:**  
- ☐ light  ☐ medium  ☐ firm  
**Areas to avoid:**  
**Areas worked:**  
**Response during session:**  
**Aftercare / home advice:**  
**Next visit / follow-up:**  

---

## Short chart-note phrases

### Intake / before session
- “No contraindications reported today.”
- “Client consented to treatment.”
- “Client prefers lighter pressure.”
- “Client requests avoidance of ___.”
- “Area(s) to avoid reviewed prior to session.”
- “Precautions discussed before starting.”

### During / after session
- “Session adjusted based on client feedback.”
- “Pressure modified for comfort.”
- “Client tolerated treatment well.”
- “Client reported the area felt less tense after work.”
- “No adverse response noted.”
- “Aftercare reviewed.”

### Aftercare / follow-up
- “Advised hydration and gentle movement.”
- “Client advised to monitor response over the next 24 hours.”
- “Follow-up as needed.”
- “Return visit recommended if symptoms persist.”
- “Client instructed to report any unusual response.”

---

## Very short fill-in example
**Date:** 4/29/26  
**Client:** ___  
**Main concern:** Neck and shoulder tension  
**Contraindications/precautions:** None reported  
**Consent obtained:** Verbal consent  
**Pressure preference:** Medium  
**Areas to avoid:** Lower back  
**Areas worked:** Neck, shoulders, upper back  
**Response during session:** Tolerated well; reported less tension  
**Aftercare:** Hydration, gentle stretching, monitor response  
**Next visit:** 1–2 weeks PRN

If you want, I can make this into an even **more compact one-page checkbox version** with almost no writing required.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s better. Still a little too roomy though — I’d want it even more stripped down, like something I could actually keep on a clipboard and fill out in 30 seconds between clients.

A few things I’d want tightened:
- fewer full sentences
- more checkbox / short-fill fields
- one line for contraindications, one for consent, one for pressure, one for areas to avoid, one for aftercare
- chart phrases that sound less generic, more like actual notes

And maybe keep it plain enough that I’m not hunting for words when I’m tired. One small-page version would be the useful thing.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a more stripped-down, clipboard-friendly version that should be quick to fill out.

## Small-page intake / follow-up note

**Date:** __________  **Client:** __________________

**Main concern:** __________________________________

**Contraindications / precautions:**  
☐ none   ☐ yes: ___________________________________

**Consent:**  
☐ verbal   ☐ written   ☐ ongoing consent confirmed

**Pressure:**  
☐ light   ☐ medium   ☐ firm   Notes: ________________

**Areas to avoid:** _________________________________

**Areas worked:** ___________________________________

**Response:** _______________________________________

**Aftercare:** _______________________________________

**Next visit:** _____________________________________

---

## Short chart phrases
A few that sound more natural in a note:

- “No contraindications reported.”
- “Consent confirmed before starting.”
- “Client requested lighter pressure.”
- “Area(s) to avoid reviewed.”
- “Focused work on ___.”
- “Pressure adjusted per client feedback.”
- “Client tolerated session well.”
- “Client noted less tension after work.”
- “Aftercare reviewed.”
- “Monitor response over the next 24 hours.”

---

## Even shorter note style
If you want to write very fast, you could use this format:

**Date/Client:** __________________  
**Concern:** ______________________  
**CI:** none / ____________________  
**Consent:** verbal / written  
**Pressure:** light / med / firm  
**Avoid:** ________________________  
**Worked:** _______________________  
**Response:** _____________________  
**Aftercare:** _____________________  

If you want, I can make this into a **true one-page template with very tight spacing** or a **printable box-style version**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the one. Tight enough. Clipboard-friendly, no fluff. The shorthand version is about right too — CI, avoid, worked, response. I could use that.

If I were tweaking it myself, I’d probably keep the chart phrases even plainer, like:
- “No CI reported.”
- “Consent confirmed.”
- “Pressure adjusted.”
- “Client tolerated well.”
- “Aftercare reviewed.”

That’s the kind of thing I’d actually write when I’m moving fast.
